articles.title: Apple’s foldable iPhone could be just around the corner
articles.description: The iOS 27 developer beta includes code that references the fold state and screen angle of a device.

articles.title: It’s not FAANG anymore. It’s MANGOS.
articles.description: With SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI all eyeing massive public debuts, the tech industry may soon have a new class of corporate overlords — and a new acronym to match. Say goodbye to FAANG and hello to MANGOS.

articles.title: Apple says it may remove some apps from the App Store if they don’t attract users
articles.description: Apple may begin removing existing apps that it considers stale, low-value, or unable to attract users.

articles.title: iOS 27 features we didn’t see onstage
articles.description: While iOS 27 isn't bringing major changes, it's still got many small features that could improve everyday usage for iPhone users.

articles.title: Apple brings streaming-style subscription bundles to the App Store
articles.description: Apple is expanding its App Bundles feature to allow developers to partner with one another on discounted subscription packages.

articles.title: Apple’s App Store rolls out personalized recommendations
articles.description: Apple will now recommend apps based on your downloads and behavior.

articles.title: Sandstone raises $30M to bring AI to in-house legal teams
articles.description: Sandstone's Series A was led by Lightspeed Partners, with participation from Sequoia.

articles.title: Lovable says it has hit $500M in annualized revenue, with 1 million new projects a week
articles.description: Lovable says it has now surpassed $500 million in annualized run-rate revenue and its users are building businesses and replacing internal software.

articles.title: How an e-scooter founder raised $5 million to build space data centers
articles.description: Orbital founder Euwyn Poon built 250,000 scooters at Spin. Now he wants to launch 10,000 space data centers.

articles.title: Evotrex raises $30M to build the RV that doesn’t need a charging station
articles.description: The startup is one of many entering the RV space, but it's banking on a hybrid power system that can go far beyond campsites.

articles.title: Zepto’s IPO filing reveals fast growth, bigger losses, and a valuation question nobody’s answered yet
articles.description: Zepto's advertising revenue jumped 151%, outpacing the company's 104% growth in operating revenue.

articles.title: Why Apple’s slow-and-steady AI bet is starting to look pretty smart
articles.description: Can Apple's new AI glow up put to bed accusations that it's losing an all-important industry race?

articles.title: Mercor’s Brendan Foody calls out Sequoia, accusing it of ‘dual-pricing’ valuation tricks
articles.description: Sequoia is just one of the top firms that sells same equity at two different prices.

articles.title: As OpenAI files for IPO, Sam Altman’s eye-scanning company is doing layoffs, report says
articles.description: Tools for Humanity, Sam Altman's identity verification company, is reportedly struggling to generate revenue and will downsize its staff.

articles.title: Apple’s WWDC AI demos looked more real after $250M false ad settlement
articles.description: The vibe of Apple's 2026 WWDC keynote felt like a spouse proudly listing all the honey-do-list items tackled. One subtle example: the many AI demos of someone standing, phone in hand.

articles.title: OpenAI files confidentially for IPO, following Anthropic
articles.description: The filing comes a little more than a week after its main rival, Anthropic, also filed to go public, ramping up the race between the two AI firms.

articles.title: Apple plays catch-up at WWDC
articles.description: Apple spent much of its WWDC keynote highlighting fixes, performance improvements, and long-requested features before unveiling its upgraded AI-powered Siri, signaling that the company wants users to see AI as just one part of a broader effort to improve its software.

articles.title: Apple bets cheaper AI will woo small developers
articles.description: As AI experimentation grows more expensive, Apple is waiving cloud API costs for developers with fewer than 2 million first-time App Store downloads.

articles.title: Waymo bought Apple’s self-driving car proving ground for $220M
articles.description: Waymo has acquired a massive 5,500-acre proving ground in Arizona owned by Route 14 Investment Partners LLC, a Delaware shell company associated with Apple, according to documents filed with Maricopa County.

articles.title: Microsoft’s open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
articles.description: Microsoft shut down dozens of GitHub code repositories for Azure and AI coding tools after a reported hack.

articles.title: US military claims first drone boat rescue of downed helicopter crew
articles.description: US Navy’s Task Force 59 achieved the drone rescue at sea near Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: High-severity vulnerability in Linux caused by a single errant character
articles.description: Use-after-free bug can be exploited to evade sandbox defenses.

articles.title: Gold isn’t inert, it just has bodyguards protecting it
articles.description: Individual gold atoms move around to form oxidation-proof structures.

articles.title: Here's Audi's next Q7 SUV and US-only SQ7, now with an RS V8
articles.description: At night, the car projects its turn signals onto the road to alert other road users.

articles.title: Apple says its AI is still private, even when it's running on Google's servers
articles.description: Some models run in Google's cloud, but without giving Google any kind of access.

articles.title: First Drive: The 2027 Rivian R2 entirely changes the EV game
articles.description: Rivian's second EV is the sub-$60,000 R2, and it was worth the wait.

articles.title: FCC lifts looming deadline for Amazon Leo satellite broadband constellation
articles.description: The waiver "serves the public interest by promoting a second large satellite broadband constellation."

articles.title: Tests suggest Russian satellites can jam GPS on a continental scale
articles.description: Mystery of GPS interference across Europe raises questions about Russian motives.

articles.title: macOS 27 requires Apple Silicon, as Apple draws down the Intel Mac era
articles.description: You'll need an M1 or better to run the next release of macOS.

articles.title: iOS 27 and iPadOS 27 don't drop support for any iPhones—and just a few iPads
articles.description: This promises to be a solid release for aging iPhones.

articles.title: Meta alleges NSO violated spyware injunction with new WhatsApp attacks
articles.description: WhatsApp disrupted spear phishing attempts, asks court to hold NSO in contempt.

articles.title: The fastest humans in the galaxy just got a spiffy patch to prove it
articles.description: "It is actually challenging how you measure [Mach] from space."

articles.title: Say hi to "Siri AI"—Apple announces new, more "conversational" voice assistant
articles.description: New features coming this fall alongside two-tiered, Google-powered AI model overhaul.

articles.title: Gemini 3.5 and Antigravity come to Google NotebookLM
articles.description: NotebookLM is getting a big upgrade, but it's only for AI Ultra and enterprise accounts right now.

articles.title: Your empty cuppa could capture carbon
articles.description: Polystyrene can be upcycled into carbon sponge material.

articles.title: For the 2nd time in weeks, Microsoft packages laced with credential stealer
articles.description: 73 packages run self-replicating stealer as soon as they're opened by an AI agent.

articles.title: Apple's iOS 27, macOS 27 Golden Gate, and other updates focus on refinement
articles.description: Apple's OSes come with Liquid Glass tweaks and performance optimizations.

articles.title: Man jailed for a month despite Flock showing he was 5 miles from crime scene
articles.description: Cop seemingly ignored Flock camera timestamp to justify arrests.

articles.title: F1 in Monaco: Finally, the cars were flat-out in qualifying
articles.description: The cars are too big to race well, but the competition for pole position is thrilling.

articles.title: A Falcon 9 booster turns 5 years old—and just set a remarkable reuse record
articles.description: We take the Falcon 9 rocket for granted. But we probably shouldn't.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **苹果折叠屏iPhone即将面世**：TechCrunch报道称苹果可折叠iPhone可能很快发布，同时苹果在AI领域的稳健策略正显现成效，WWDC上的AI演示在支付2.5亿美元虚假广告和解后显得更加真实。
2. **苹果App Store多项重大调整**：包括可能移除长期不吸引用户的App、推出类似流媒体的订阅捆绑服务、以及上线个性化推荐功能，iOS 27还披露了未在发布会上展示的新特性。
3. **科技公司融资与上市动态**：Sandstone获3000万美元用于法律AI、Lovable年化收入达5亿美元且每周新增100万项目、电动滑板车创始人融资500万美元建太空数据中心、Evotrex融资3000万美元开发无需充电站的房车，Zepto上市文件显示快速增长但亏损扩大。
4. **行业争议与人事变动**：Mercor创始人指责红杉资本采用“双重定价”估值策略，OpenAI申请IPO之际，Sam Altman的虹膜扫描公司正在裁员。
5. **市场格局变化**：TechCrunch提出“MANGOS”（微软、苹果、英伟达、谷歌、甲骨文、索尼）可能取代传统的FAANG成为科技巨头新代称。

今日最值得关注的发展：苹果App Store正经历重大商业模式变革，从应用审核到推荐机制、订阅捆绑及用户活跃度要求，或将重塑开发者生态与用户体验。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **Hugging Face多项技术更新**：包括通过链式调用两个Spaces构建3D巴黎画廊的Agent、社区支持用于智能体强化学习的OpenEnv框架、Nemotron 3.5内容安全模型（支持企业级多模态安全定制）、EVA-Bench数据2.0（覆盖3领域、121工具、213场景），以及为Hub设计的Agent优化版hf CLI工具。

2. **OpenAI发布多项进展**：包括向SEC秘密提交S-1草案（IPO进程推进）、发布“造福所有人”计划、推出经济研究交流平台、与Endava合作以AI Agent重新定义软件交付、通过“Dreaming”技术提升ChatGPT记忆能力，以及探讨情报时代的生物防御。

3. **Google AI与搜索功能更新**：宣布2026年5月最新AI新闻，并推出5种利用Google搜索提升二手与复古购物体验的方法。

4. **AI研究方向拓展**：Hugging Face提出将直接偏好优化（DPO）应用于聊天机器人之外的领域，并展示了为Reachy Mini机器人添加MCP工具的实际案例。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：OpenAI正式启动IPO进程并公布社会责任计划，同时Hugging Face密集发布多项Agent与安全相关技术，显示AI行业正加速从模型研发向Agent生态与企业级应用落地转型。
